steps.title: Identify what weakens the claim
steps.content: The student claims that scat-detecting dogs produces the smallest difference between minimum and maximum densities. To weaken this claim, we need evidence that scat-detecting dogs does not have the smallest difference.

steps.title: Match the evidence to the choices
steps.content:
If Gregory A. Davidson et al. used scat-detecting dogs and their reported density range was greater than the ranges from studies using other methods, this directly contradicts the student's claim by showing scat-detecting dogs lacks the smallest difference.

Options A and D report isolated data points without comparative context; B compares two studies without clarifying which method was used or the actual ranges involved.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
